title: Handroanthus riodocensis
intro: Handroanthus riodocensis, also known as the Rio Doce tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Handroanthus riodocensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Handroanthus riodocensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health.
taxonomy: Handroanthus riodocensis is classified within the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as the bignonia or trumpet vine family. This family consists of around 140 genera and 1,000 species of flowering plants. Handroanthus riodocensis is a member of the genus Handroanthus, which is native to South America. The genus Handroanthus is a group of flowering plants that are often known as lapacho or tabebuia trees. These plants are characterized by their showy flowers and large, deciduous leaves.
